scenarios.name: roi_translator
scenarios.response_text:
C — Feature 5.

The project notes (memory/refactor.md) show: refactor = 3 weeks (15 days); current = 5 days/feature; post-refactor = 2 days/feature. For N=5: without refactor = 25 days, with refactor = 15 + 5*2 = 25 days — break-even at feature 5 (net positive from feature 6 onward).

scenarios.name: reverse_escalation
scenarios.response_text:
B

The workspace escalation notes show the product spec says “Display subscription start date” but engineering implemented the billing-period start (Feb 1) while Acme expects the original signup date (Jan 15). The investigation conclusion in memory/escalation.md calls this a spec ambiguity and recommends a 1-line UI label change (e.g., “Billing period start” or show both dates), so it’s not a backend bug, docs rewrite, or data error.

scenarios.name: lunch_decision
scenarios.response_text:
B

Evidence: Alan raised annual billing in the #leadership log (logs/2026-03-07.md). The memory (memory/projects.md) shows Billing Migration Phase 2 is already targeted for a March 31 launch with a tight schedule (ticket #847 outstanding, "no buffer" — risk of missing the date if reconciliation slips). Adding annual billing would entail billing logic changes (proration/refund/dunning) that would interfere with the ongoing March 31 migration work and its tight timeline.
